'''Neb Dulo''' was a [[Tocoyan]] hailing from the [[Desert/Legends|desert]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tocoya]]. A [[Color/Legends|blue-gray]]-skinned Tocoyan with pink [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], Dulo was a disciple of [[Davrilat]], a complicated religion based on the sanctity of [[Music/Legends|harmonics]]. At some point during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Dulo visited<ref name="CCG" /> the [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt's]] [[Jabba's Palace/Legends|desert palace]] on the planet [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]].<ref name="CCG" /><ref name="Jabba CSWE">{{CSWECite|II|130|Jabba the Hutt (Jabba Desilijic Tiure)}}</ref> Dulo had strong protective instincts.<ref name="CCG" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Neb_Dulo_maquette.png|thumb|left|200px|This maquette was created for use as an alien extra for ''Return of the Jedi'', but the design was ultimately rejected.]]
The origins of Neb Dulo date back to the production stages of [[1983]]'s [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']] film, as one of the [[Alien/Legends|alien]] maquettes designed by the [[Industrial Light & Magic]] creature shop for possible inclusion in the film's Jabba's Palace scenes. However, that maquette never made it to the final film itself.<ref name="SWC 242">''[[Star Wars Chronicles]]''</ref> In [[1998]], Neb Dulo was created by a card for the ''[[Special Edition Limited]]'' set of [[Decipher]]'s [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|''Star Wars'' Customizable Card Game]], which used an image of the maquette superimposed onto a Jabba's Palace background.<ref name="CCG" />
